experiment: Maass coefficient distribution shape vs level
method: Pool c_p (p prime, p not dividing level, |c_p|<=2 for tempered) per level, compare to Sato-Tate semicircle (2/pi)sqrt(1-x^2/4)

conclusions.kurtosis_trend: No significant kurtosis trend with level (slope=0.000219, p=0.9830)
conclusions.prime_vs_composite: Prime levels do NOT fit semicircle better (KS: prime=0.00813 vs composite=0.00656)
conclusions.spectral_dependence: Shape does NOT significantly depend on spectral parameter (2-sample KS p=2.32e-01)
conclusions.tempered_fraction: 2586311/2586455 c_p values tempered (99.994%). Raw M4/M2^2=59.0704 vs tempered M4/M2^2=2.0209 -- non-tempered forms severely distort moments
conclusions.systematic_deviation: Systematic deviation detected: 100/100 bins significant. Pattern: center=-0.31152, mid=-0.26954, tail=-0.14678. Deficit at center relative to tails
conclusions.verdict: CONFIRMED: Maass form c_p follow Sato-Tate semicircle at every tested level. M4/M2^2=2.0209 matches SU(2) prediction 2.0 within 0.0209. Shape is universal across levels.
